displayTitle: The Three Levers You Actually Control

beats.narration: The part hands you conductivity, permeability, and shape — all fixed. The test system hands you three levers: frequency, coupling, and field strength.

payload.title: THE THREE LEVERS
payload.sub: Frequency, coupling, and drive — your half of the equation.

beats.narration: Frequency is the master lever. The operating point follows the product of frequency, conductivity, permeability, and diameter — Förster's f over f-g ratio.

payload.heading: Frequency places the operating point
payload.note: EQUAL f/fg → SAME POINT · THE LAW OF SIMILARITY

beats.narration: Walk the point down the curve and lift-off, conductivity, and crack signals split apart. The price is penetration — depth shrinks as frequency climbs.
Coupling sets how much field reaches the part: lift-off on surface probes, fill factor — diameter ratio squared — in coils. Drive scales every signal.
But steel breaks the rule: permeability rides the drive level. Saturate the part, or lock the drive and never touch it.

payload.sub: In steel, every drive setting is a different test.
